subject: install controlloop drools applications
commit_message:
install controlloop drools applications

it will get the proper control loop application  on the fly
to minimize space used in configmaps.

upgrades mariadb to latest stable version (previous one
used by policy was outdated).

fixes pessimistic lock DB problems on PE side

fixes outdated policy-keystore overriding the image one

Change-Id: Ia78d23b57b4e314c44c97912d544b85d0d6c9c5e
Issue-ID: POLICY-766
Signed-off-by: Jorge Hernandez <jh1730@att.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.
Uploaded patch set 2: Commit message was updated.
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Hi Jorge,

I suggest that do-start.sh script will be common and we can use same for drools chart.

so may be we can put a case in common do-start.sh file and use in drools.

Thanks,
MG

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Hello Mayank, regarding do-start.sh.   I believe it best to remove it so not to shadow the official do-start.sh from the policy/drools-pdp itself.   It was out of sync, and it is the case as I tested it that the official one works with both heat and oom installation.  I think shadowing the official copy should be last resort, to avoid maintenance of multiple copies.   Some cases may have to be done, I suppose that's the reason you introduced it in the PE side, but is not the current case for pdp-d.
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

> (1 comment)
 > 
 > Hello Mayank, regarding do-start.sh.   I believe it best to remove
 > it so not to shadow the official do-start.sh from the
 > policy/drools-pdp itself.   It was out of sync, and it is the case
 > as I tested it that the official one works with both heat and oom
 > installation.  I think shadowing the official copy should be last
 > resort, to avoid maintenance of multiple copies.   Some cases may
 > have to be done, I suppose that's the reason you introduced it in
 > the PE side, but is not the current case for pdp-d.

Hi Jorge,

If you remember, we had a discussion around this. 
In the do-start.sh file in the image, there are checks for the ports to be up for mariadb which is hard coded name and it will fail if service name of mariadb is changed in future.
Thats why I had overridden this file and removed the checks for port to be up as K8s starts the dependent pods only when initcontainer is done.

If you still want to keep the do-start.sh from image then I would suggest to pass an env variable with service name of mariadb (and other services) and use that in the scripts.
There are 2 do-start.sh scripts as of now in oom and both can be removed if you take care of these wait-for-port thing.

Thx,
Mahendra

Patch Set 2:

Mahendra, that's not the case anymore for drools, it is safe to remove (and it works per my testing) with the SNAPSHOT images.   See the official one (in master) 

https://git.onap.org/policy/drools-pdp/tree/packages/docker/src/main/docker/do-start.sh

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

> Mahendra, that's not the case anymore for drools, it is safe to
 > remove (and it works per my testing) with the SNAPSHOT images.  
 > See the official one (in master)
 > 
 > https://git.onap.org/policy/drools-pdp/tree/packages/docker/src/main/docker/do-start.sh

Ok great then.
Uploaded patch set 3.
Uploaded patch set 4.
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 4:

Jorge, can you please verify that you have tested this change in our lab?

Patch Set 4:

Yes, this is all tested .. Only caveat if reusing an existing lab installations is that the dockerdata-nfs contains persistent storage for previous mariadb installation in an earlier version, if that's the case in the testing environment, the corresponding dockerdata-nfs/onap/.. should be deleted previous to bring up.

Jorge, there may not be a problem but checking health with curl isn't the same as actually running the Robot health check.  There are robot config files that are unique to OOM so even verifying on Heat isn't enough.

Patch Set 4:

Hello Roger, just for clarity, the curl command was on my oom lab installation (not heat).   I disabled robot and other components to speed up things and save some resources.   I'll provide output at some point then for robot, it should work fine though.   In any case, I may take advantage and piggyback some other changes to this review.

Patch Set 4:

Jorge, I think that is sufficient I'll let Mike/Roger confirm.

The OOM team is too overwhelmed right now. What they would like is for teams to test the patch in their environment first using Robot. If good, you can +1 the verification. In Casablanca we hope to have a CD process that will do the verify for teams.
Uploaded patch set 5.

Patch Set 5:

Uploaded a last set of changes which is putting us in some decent shape for policy.   Miscellaneous problems fixed, almost ready for use case scenario testing.  There's an issue with dmaap, that I think probably is because I'm using older image.

I would like this to be merged if everybody is OK since there's quite a bit of important changes.

Tested in my environment per output above.
